_meta.description: Side-by-side comparison of Tasklet v2 (31 tools) vs Sunder equivalents
_meta.purpose: Walk through each tool to align Sunder's wording/schema with Tasklet's battle-tested definitions
_meta.resume_note: ALL 31 tools reviewed. 5 PAUSED: Tool 4 (read_file) — multimodal PR, Tool 5 (write_file) — absolute paths PR, Tools 9-10 (run_agent_memory_sql, get_agent_db_schema) — CRM schema decisions, Tool 11 (send_email) — Resend integration. 10 DEFERRED (not in v1 scope): Tools 3, 12-14, 26-30. Remaining 16 tools all DECIDED. Next: revisit paused tools as their blocking PRs land, then address Sunder-only tools (search_knowledge, ask_user_question, 28 CRM tools).

tasklet.name: web_scrape_website
tasklet.description: Reads a single webpage and extracts its content as markdown

url.description: The URL of the webpage to scrape. Must be either a http:// or https:// URL.

sunder.name: web_scrape
sunder.description: Read a webpage and extract its text content. Use this to read articles, documentation, or any web page.

url.description: The URL of the webpage to read. Must be http:// or https://.

diffs.tasklet: web_scrape_website
diffs.note: Sunder shorter name

diffs.tasklet: Reads a single webpage and extracts its content as markdown
diffs.sunder: Read a webpage and extract its text content. Use this to read articles, documentation, or any web page.
diffs.note: Tasklet is more precise ('as markdown'). Sunder adds usage guidance ('Use this to...')

diffs.tasklet: The URL of the webpage to scrape. Must be either a http:// or https:// URL.
diffs.sunder: The URL of the webpage to read. Must be http:// or https://.
diffs.note: Nearly identical

decision.new_description: Reads a single webpage and extracts its content as markdown
decision.rationale: Adopt Tasklet's precise description ('as markdown'). Drop 'Use this to...' hand-holding. Keep shorter name.

tasklet.name: web_search_web
tasklet.description: Searches the web for relevant content based on a query

query.description: The search query to use for web search

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 10, max: 100)

location.description: Geographic location for search results. Examples: "Germany", "San Francisco,California,United States". Default: "US"

tbs.description: Time-based search parameter. Use predefined values: qdr:h (hour), qdr:d (day), qdr:w (week), qdr:m (month), qdr:y (year). Or custom date range: cdr:1,cd_min:MM/DD/YYYY,cd_max:MM/DD/YYYY

sunder.name: web_search
sunder.description: Search the web for current information. Returns titles, URLs, and snippets.

limit.description: Maximum results to return. Defaults to 10, max 20.

location.description: Country code to target results. Supported: AR, AU, AT, BE, BR, CA, CL, CN, DK, FI, FR, DE, GR, HK, IN, ID, IT, JP, KR, MY, MX, NL, NZ, NO, PH, PL, PT, RU, SA, ZA, ES, SE, CH, TW, TR, GB, US. Omit for global results.

tbs.description: Time filter shortcuts: qdr:h, qdr:d, qdr:w, qdr:m, qdr:y.

diffs.tasklet: web_search_web
diffs.note: Sunder shorter name

diffs.tasklet: Searches the web for relevant content based on a query
diffs.sunder: Search the web for current information. Returns titles, URLs, and snippets.
diffs.note: Sunder adds return shape guidance
Sunder caps at 20 (Brave API limit). Tasklet allows 100.

diffs.tasklet: Free-text geographic ('Germany', 'San Francisco,California,United States')
diffs.note: Different location format due to different search backends

diffs.tasklet: Includes custom date range: cdr:1,cd_min:MM/DD/YYYY,cd_max:MM/DD/YYYY
diffs.note: Sunder missing custom date range param docs

decision.rationale: Sunder's description is arguably better (tells model what comes back). All param diffs are backend-driven (Brave API constraints), not wording issues.

tasklet.name: run_command
tasklet.description: Executes shell commands in the sandbox environment.

action_pending.description: Custom UI status text shown while running.

action_finished.description: Custom UI status text shown on success.

action_error.description: Custom UI status text shown on failure.

command.description: The shell command to execute in the sandbox environment.

diffs.note: NOT IMPLEMENTED. Sunder has no sandbox/shell execution tool. Planned for v2 plan Phase 3+.

decision.rationale: Sandbox/shell execution is Phase 3+. Nothing to align now.

tasklet.name: read_file
tasklet.description: Reads the contents of a file or directory by its path. If the path is a directory, returns a recursive tree-style listing of its contents. Image files are displayed directly. For PDF files, returns pages as images by default to preserve visual layout, formatting, and diagrams. Use format='text' to extract text content only. Specify optional start_line/end_line or start_page/end_page for large files. Use negative indices to count from the end (e.g., start_line: -10, end_line: -1 reads the last 10 lines).

path.description: Absolute path to the file or directory to view (e.g., '/agent/home' or '/tmp/file.txt')

start_line.description: For text files only. The line to start reading from (1-indexed). Use negative numbers to count from the end.

end_line.description: For text files only. The line to stop reading at (1-indexed, inclusive). Use negative numbers to count from the end.

pdf_start_page.description: For PDF files only. The page to start reading from (1-indexed).

pdf_end_page.description: For PDF files only. The page to stop reading at (1-indexed, inclusive).

pdf_format.description: For PDF files only. Specifies whether to return pages as images or text. Defaults to 'image'.

sunder.name: read_file
sunder.description: Read file content or list a directory tree. Use directory paths (e.g. memory/) for discovery.

path.description: Relative file or directory path in the client workspace.

diffs.tasklet: (long, feature-rich description covering directories, images, PDFs, negative indices)
diffs.sunder: Read file content or list a directory tree. Use directory paths (e.g. memory/) for discovery.
diffs.note: Sunder description is minimal. Tasklet's is much more instructive about capabilities.

diffs.tasklet: Absolute path (/agent/home)
diffs.note: Different path semantics — Tasklet uses absolute, Sunder uses workspace-relative

diffs.tasklet: Supports negative indices (-1 = last line)
diffs.note: Sunder doesn't support negative line indices

diffs.tasklet: Has pdf_start_page, pdf_end_page, pdf_format
diffs.note: Sunder has no PDF support

decision.new_description: Reads the contents of a file or directory by its path. If the path is a directory, returns a recursive tree-style listing of its contents. Image files and PDFs are displayed directly. Specify optional start_line/end_line for large text files. Use negative indices to count from the end (e.g., start_line: -10, end_line: -1 reads the last 10 lines).

param_changes.action: KEEP_SUNDER — absolute path examples are better than Tasklet's generic wording

param_changes.new_describe: For text files only. The line to start reading from (1-indexed). Use negative numbers to count from the end (-1 = last line, -10 = 10th from last). Defaults to 1 if not specified.
For text files only. The line to stop reading at (1-indexed, inclusive). Use negative numbers to count from the end (-1 = last line). Defaults to end of file if not specified.

param_changes.action: SKIP — Sunder sends whole PDF as raw bytes, no page-level control or text extraction mode

decision.rationale: Copy Tasklet description but drop format='text' and start_page/end_page sentences (Sunder sends whole PDF as bytes). Keep Sunder's path description (absolute path examples). Copy Tasklet's richer start_line/end_line descriptions with negative index examples.

tasklet.name: write_file
tasklet.description: Creates, edits, or deletes a file in the filesystem. Supports three operations: write (create or overwrite), edit (find and replace text), and delete.

action_pending.description: Custom UI status text shown while running.

action_finished.description: Custom UI status text shown on success.

action_error.description: Custom UI status text shown on failure.

old_string.description: Exact text to find (required for edit op)

sunder.name: write_file
sunder.description: Write, edit, or delete files in the client workspace.

path.description: Relative file path in the client workspace.

diffs.tasklet: Creates, edits, or deletes a file in the filesystem. Supports three operations: write (create or overwrite), edit (find and replace text), and delete.
diffs.sunder: Write, edit, or delete files in the client workspace.
diffs.note: Tasklet more descriptive about what each op does

diffs.tasklet: Has action_pending, action_finished, action_error (required)
diffs.note: Sunder has no UI status params. These are Tasklet's streaming UI feature.

diffs.tasklet: minLength: 1
diffs.note: Tasklet prevents empty string matches

decision.new_description: Creates, edits, or deletes a file in the filesystem. Supports three operations: write (create or overwrite), edit (find and replace text), and delete.

param_changes.action: KEEP_SUNDER — absolute path examples are better than Tasklet's generic 'Path for the file'

param_changes.new_describe: File content, overwrites existing content (required for write op)
Exact text to find and replace in the file (required for edit op)
Replacement text, can be empty to delete old_string (required for edit op)
If true, replace all occurrences. If false (default), fails on multiple matches.

decision.rationale: Copy Tasklet description verbatim. Copy all param descriptions from Tasklet except path (keep Sunder's absolute path examples). Skip action_* params. Add .min(1) to old_string.

tasklet.name: run_subagent
tasklet.description:
Runs a subagent to handle work efficiently outside of your main context. Returns the final message from the subagent as its result.
Running subagents reduces costs and keeps your context focused. This is especially useful when you are doing similar work multiple times.

The subagent receives the content of the markdown file followed by any payload data you provide in the first user message.

Before running a subagent, consider whether the subagent's approach still fits the current situation - you can always update its file with write_file if needed.

path.description: Full path to the subagent markdown file

payload.description: Optional data to pass to the subagent

diffs.note: NOT IMPLEMENTED. Subagent infrastructure planned for later phases.

decision.new_description:
Runs a subagent to handle work efficiently outside of your main context. Returns the final message from the subagent as its result.
Running subagents reduces costs and keeps your context focused. This is especially useful when you are doing similar work multiple times.

The subagent receives the content of the markdown file followed by any payload data you provide in the first user message.

Before running a subagent, consider whether the subagent's approach still fits the current situation - you can always update its file with write_file if needed.

param_changes.add_describe: Custom UI status text shown while running. IMPORTANT: Output these three action_ parameters before all other parameters.
Custom UI status text shown on success.
Custom UI status text shown on failure.
Full path to the subagent markdown file (e.g., "/agent/subagents/email_processor.md")
Optional data to pass to the subagent that will be added after the subagent's instructions in the first user message. This allows the same subagent to process different inputs.

decision.rationale: Sunder has the right skeleton but zero param descriptions. Copy Tasklet's full description and all param descriptions verbatim.

tasklet.name: manage_tasks
tasklet.description:
Manage task items for this agent. Supports batch operations for efficiency.

Operations:
- add: Create a new task with a title and optional payload. taskId MUST NOT be set.
- update: Modify an existing task's title or payload. taskId is REQUIRED.
- delete: Remove a task to mark it as done. taskId is REQUIRED.

You can perform multiple operations in a single call (e.g., add multiple tasks, update several at once, or mix different operations).

Note: All current tasks are visible in the agent's synced state.

taskId.description: Required for update/delete, MUST NOT be set for add

sunder.name: manage_todo
sunder.description: Manage thread-scoped scratchpad todos. Supports add, update, and delete operations in one call.

diffs.tasklet: manage_tasks
diffs.note: Different name — 'tasks' vs 'todo'

diffs.tasklet: Rich description with operation docs, batch guidance, and synced state note
diffs.sunder: Manage thread-scoped scratchpad todos. Supports add, update, and delete operations in one call.
diffs.note: Tasklet much more instructive

diffs.tasklet: operation
diffs.note: Different field name for the operation type

diffs.tasklet: taskId
diffs.note: Different ID field name

diffs.tasklet: no max
diffs.note: Sunder caps batch at 20

decision.new_description:
Manage todo items for this agent. Supports batch operations for efficiency.

Operations:
- add: Create a new todo with a title and optional payload. todo_id MUST NOT be set.
- update: Modify an existing todo's title or payload. todo_id is REQUIRED.
- delete: Remove a todo to mark it as done. todo_id is REQUIRED.

You can perform multiple operations in a single call (e.g., add multiple todos, update several at once, or mix different operations).

Note: All current todos are visible in the agent's synced state.

param_changes.new_describe: Array of todo operations to perform. You can add, update, or delete multiple todos in a single call.
The operation to perform: add (create new todo, todo_id must NOT be set), update (modify existing todo), delete (remove todo to mark it as done)
The ID of the todo. REQUIRED for update and delete operations. MUST NOT be set for add operation.
The title of the todo (required for add, optional for update)
Optional JSON payload attached to the todo for additional information in addition to the title

decision.rationale: Copy Tasklet verbatim with find-replace: taskId→todo_id, task→todo, operation→op. Keep manage_todo name to avoid confusion with CRM tasks.

tasklet.name: list_tasks
tasklet.description: List tasks for this agent. Can optionally filter by specific task IDs.

taskIds.description: Optional array of task IDs to filter.

sunder.name: list_todo
sunder.description: List open todos for the current thread.

ids.description: Optional list of todo UUIDs to include.

diffs.tasklet: list_tasks
List tasks for this agent. Can optionally filter by specific task IDs.
diffs.sunder: List open todos for the current thread.
diffs.note: Sunder scopes to thread, Tasklet to agent

diffs.tasklet: taskIds

decision.new_description: List todos for this agent. Can optionally filter by specific todo IDs.

param_changes.new_describe: Optional array of todo IDs to filter. If not provided, returns all todos.

decision.rationale: Copy Tasklet verbatim with task→todo, taskIds→ids name swap.

tasklet.name: run_agent_memory_sql
tasklet.description: Runs a SQL query against the agent's SQL database.

query.description: The SQL query to execute. Must be a single SQL statement.

sunder.name: run_agent_memory_sql
sunder.description: Run a single read-only SQL query against client-accessible tables. Use get_agent_db_schema first to inspect available schema.

diffs.note: IDENTICAL

diffs.tasklet: Runs a SQL query against the agent's SQL database.
diffs.sunder: Run a single read-only SQL query against client-accessible tables. Use get_agent_db_schema first to inspect available schema.
diffs.note: Sunder adds read-only constraint and cross-references get_agent_db_schema. Tasklet's is generic (doesn't say read-only).

diffs.tasklet: The SQL query to execute. Must be a single SQL statement.
diffs.note: Sunder explicitly restricts to SELECT/CTE. Tasklet says 'single statement' (implies any DML?).

decision.rationale: CRM tool schema still being decided. SQL tool description depends on what tables/access model we land on. Resume after CRM tool pruning decisions.

tasklet.name: get_agent_db_schema
tasklet.description: Retrieves the complete schema of the agent's SQL database, including all table definitions, column types, and row counts.

sunder.name: get_agent_db_schema
sunder.description: Get available tables, columns, and row counts for the agent SQL workspace.

diffs.note: IDENTICAL

diffs.tasklet: Retrieves the complete schema of the agent's SQL database, including all table definitions, column types, and row counts.
diffs.sunder: Get available tables, columns, and row counts for the agent SQL workspace.
diffs.note: Tasklet slightly more precise ('complete schema', 'column types')

decision.rationale: Tied to CRM schema decisions. Resume with Tool 9.

tasklet.name: send_message
tasklet.description:
Send a message to the user or other verified contact methods via:
- Email
- Text

Messages will come from an email address or phone number associated with this agent.

Use 'owner' as the recipient to send to the user's primary email (always available). For other addresses, they must be verified first using add_contact_method.

For replies to existing threads, use reply_message instead.

to.description: Recipients. Use 'owner' for account owner's primary email. Cannot mix email and text.

body.description: Message body. Supports markdown for email.

subject.description: Required for email, disallowed for others.

sunder.name: send_message
sunder.description: Send a message to the user or another verified contact method. Use 'owner' to target the account owner's primary email. This environment currently logs the intent but does not deliver the message.

to.description: Recipients. Use 'owner' for the primary user email.

body.description: Message body. Supports markdown for email.

diffs.note: IDENTICAL

diffs.tasklet: Rich multi-line with channel types, owner shortcut, verification note, and reply_message cross-ref
diffs.sunder: Shorter, includes stub warning 'does not deliver the message'
diffs.note: Sunder is a stub. When delivery is wired, should adopt Tasklet's full description.

diffs.tasklet: Detailed: cannot mix email and text recipients
diffs.note: Sunder should add the mixing constraint

diffs.tasklet: Required for email, disallowed for others
diffs.note: Tasklet clarifies when subject is required vs disallowed

decision.rationale: Stub tool — need to wire Resend for email delivery first. After that: copy Tasklet's description and all param descriptions verbatim, drop stub warning.

tasklet.name: reply_message
tasklet.description: Reply to an existing message thread. For email, this sends a reply-all. For text, this continues the same conversation.

messageId.description: The message ID to reply to.

diffs.note: NOT IMPLEMENTED. Depends on messaging infrastructure (Phase 3+).

decision.rationale: Depends on Resend/messaging infra. Build when send_message gets wired.

tasklet.name: list_contact_methods
tasklet.description: List all available contact methods. Returns the user's primary email (use 'owner' as recipient) and any verified additional contact methods.

diffs.note: NOT IMPLEMENTED. Depends on messaging infrastructure.

decision.rationale: Depends on Resend/messaging infra. Build when send_message gets wired.

tasklet.name: add_contact_method
tasklet.description: Add a new contact method. Supported types: Email, Text. Contact methods must be verified before sending. Verification: Email clicks link, Text sends a message. Owner's email always available without verification.

value.description: Email address or E.164 phone number

name.description: Label for this contact method, used as sender name

diffs.note: NOT IMPLEMENTED. Depends on messaging infrastructure.

decision.rationale: Depends on Resend/messaging infra. Build when send_message gets wired.

tasklet.name: search_triggers
tasklet.description:
Search for available triggers by keywords.
Returns a list of trigger types that match the search criteria, along with their setup schemas and any prerequisites.

Use this tool to discover what triggers are available before setting one up.

The setupSchema field of each returned trigger describes the schema of the params object that should be passed into the setup_trigger tool.

Triggers that support editing will include an editSchema field describing the parameters for the edit action in manage_active_triggers.

keywords.description: One or more keywords to search for available triggers

sunder.name: search_triggers
sunder.description: Search available trigger types by keyword and return the setup schema and edit schema for each match.

diffs.note: IDENTICAL

diffs.tasklet: Multi-line with workflow guidance: discover before setup, setupSchema explains params, editSchema for manage_active_triggers
diffs.sunder: Single line: 'Search available trigger types by keyword and return the setup schema and edit schema for each match.'
diffs.note: Tasklet's description guides the agent through the full trigger workflow. Sunder's is minimal.

decision.new_description:
Search for available triggers by keywords.
Returns a list of trigger types that match the search criteria, along with their setup schemas and any prerequisites.

Use this tool to discover what triggers are available before setting one up.

The setupSchema field of each returned trigger describes the schema of the params object that should be passed into the setup_trigger tool.

Triggers that support editing will include an editSchema field describing the parameters for the edit action in manage_active_triggers.

param_changes.new_describe: One or more keywords to search for available triggers (e.g., ["email", "schedule"])

decision.rationale: Copy Tasklet verbatim. Full workflow guidance for the model.

tasklet.name: setup_trigger
tasklet.description:
Set up a new trigger instance. First use search_triggers to find available triggers and their setup schemas, then call this tool with the trigger ID and required parameters.
On completion, shows the user a UI card with the trigger details.

trigger_id.description: The ID of the trigger type to set up

params.description: Setup parameters as defined by the trigger's setupSchema

invocation_message.description: Optional short message included each time trigger runs

sunder.name: setup_trigger
sunder.description: Create a new trigger instance for the current thread using the trigger ID and params returned by search_triggers.

name.note: EXTRA: Sunder requires a name field Tasklet doesn't

instruction_path.note: EXTRA: Sunder requires instruction_path Tasklet doesn't

diffs.note: IDENTICAL

diffs.tasklet: Includes workflow guidance and UI card mention
diffs.note: Tasklet cross-references search_triggers

diffs.tasklet: N/A
diffs.note: Sunder has extra 'name' param Tasklet doesn't

diffs.tasklet: N/A
diffs.note: Sunder has extra 'instruction_path' param Tasklet doesn't — ties trigger to a file with instructions

decision.new_description:
Set up a new trigger instance. First use search_triggers to find available triggers and their setup schemas, then call this tool with the trigger ID and required parameters.
On completion, shows the user a UI card with the trigger details.

param_changes.new_describe: The ID of the trigger type to set up (e.g., "schedule", "webhook", "rss")
A human-readable name for this trigger instance.
Path to the instruction file the agent should follow when this trigger fires.
Setup parameters as defined by the trigger's setupSchema
Optional short message that is included each time this trigger runs. Max 200 characters.

decision.rationale: Copy Tasklet description verbatim. Add descriptions to all params including Sunder-only ones (name, instruction_path).

tasklet.name: manage_active_triggers
tasklet.description:
Manage the agent's active triggers.

Actions:
- list: Returns all active triggers
- view: Shows detailed info. Requires trigger_instance_id.
- delete: Removes a trigger. Destructive.
- simulate: Fires a test event. Requires payload.
- edit: Modifies config. Requires edit_params and/or invocation_message.

sunder.name: manage_active_triggers
sunder.description: List, inspect, edit, delete, or simulate active user-created triggers for the current client.

diffs.note: IDENTICAL

diffs.tasklet: Multi-line with per-action docs and requirements
diffs.note: Tasklet's per-action documentation is much more instructive for the model
Schema is functionally equivalent. Sunder uses z.string().uuid() for trigger_instance_id (stricter).

decision.new_description:
Manage the agent's active triggers.

Actions:
- list: Returns all active triggers with their IDs, names, titles, invocationMessage, and arguments.
- view: Shows detailed information for a specific trigger. Requires trigger_instance_id.
- delete: Removes an active trigger. Requires trigger_instance_id. This is destructive.
- simulate: Fires a test event on a trigger to test the agent's response. Requires trigger_instance_id and payload.
- edit: Modifies an existing trigger's configuration. Requires trigger_instance_id. Use edit_params to modify trigger configuration (matching editSchema, not supported by all triggers) and/or invocation_message to set or clear the invocation title override.

Use list first to see available triggers and get their instance IDs.
decision.rationale: Copy Tasklet verbatim. Per-action docs are critical for the model. Params already equivalent.

tasklet.name: list_users_connections
tasklet.description:
Lists all connections to external services that the user has already created in their account.
Returns connectionId, serviceName, description, accountName, connectionType, and connection-specific details for each connection.

sunder.name: list_users_connections
sunder.description: Lists all connections to external services that the user has already created in their account. Returns active, inactive, error, and pending connections with connectionId, serviceName, description, accountName, connectionType, and connection-specific details.

diffs.note: IDENTICAL
Nearly identical. Sunder adds 'active, inactive, error, and pending' status clarification.

decision.rationale: Sunder's description is already good — slightly better than Tasklet's with status types.

tasklet.name: get_details_for_connections
tasklet.description:
Gets detailed information for the listed connections.
Returns a full list of tools, including both activated and deactivated tools, for each connection, including full detailed descriptions and arguments if requested.
Also returns connectionId, serviceName, description, accountName, connectionType, toolCount, and other connection-specific details.

Use this to:
- Discover what actions you can perform with a connection before activating it
- Check which tools are already activated for a connection
- Verify exact tool names before activating connections

includeToolDetails.description: Pass true to include detailed descriptions and arguments

sunder.name: get_details_for_connections
sunder.description: Gets detailed information for the listed connections. Returns a full list of tools, including both activated and deactivated tools, for each connection, including full descriptions and arguments if requested. Use this to discover what actions are available before activating tools.

diffs.note: IDENTICAL

diffs.tasklet: Includes 3-bullet 'Use this to' guide
diffs.note: Tasklet more instructive with specific use cases

decision.new_description:
Gets detailed information for the listed connections.
Returns a full list of tools, including both activated and deactivated tools, for each connection, including full detailed descriptions and arguments if requested.
Also returns connectionId, serviceName, description, accountName, connectionType, toolCount, and other connection-specific details.

Use this to:
- Discover what actions you can perform with a connection before activating it
- Check which tools are already activated for a connection
- Verify exact tool names before activating connections
decision.rationale: Copy Tasklet verbatim. 3-bullet use-case guide helps the model know when to reach for this tool.

tasklet.name: get_integrations_capabilities
tasklet.description: Lists the capabilities available via the given integrations, including tools (if available), quality information (GREAT, GOOD, OK, LIMITED, and UNKNOWN), and notes.

sunder.name: get_integrations_capabilities
sunder.description: Lists the capabilities available via the given integrations, including tools, quality information, and notes.

diffs.note: IDENTICAL

diffs.tasklet: Enumerates quality levels: GREAT, GOOD, OK, LIMITED, UNKNOWN
diffs.note: Minor — Tasklet spells out the quality levels

decision.new_description: Lists the capabilities available via the given integrations, including tools (if available), quality information (GREAT, GOOD, OK, LIMITED, and UNKNOWN), and notes.
decision.rationale: Copy Tasklet verbatim. Quality levels are real values in the response.

tasklet.name: search_for_integrations
tasklet.description:
Lists integrations that match one or more given keywords. Keywords are single words.
Searches integrations built by the Tasklet team as well as integrations from Pipedream (over 3000 total).

NEVER mention integration quality scores or who built the integrations unless the user specifically asks.

Once you have the integration ID you can get more info using get_integrations_capabilities.

keywords.description: Single-word keywords. Verify exact tool names by calling get_integrations_capabilities first.

sunder.name: search_for_integrations
sunder.description: Lists integrations that match one or more given keywords. Keywords are single words like email, billing, tasks, Gmail, or Slack. Returns integration ID, name, description, quality score, builder, and context. NEVER mention quality or builder unless the user specifically asks.

diffs.note: IDENTICAL

diffs.tasklet: References Pipedream and get_integrations_capabilities cross-ref
diffs.sunder: Lists return fields explicitly, has NEVER mention rule
diffs.note: Both have the NEVER mention rule. Tasklet adds cross-reference to get_integrations_capabilities. Sunder references Composio catalog instead of Pipedream.

decision.new_description:
Lists integrations that match one or more given keywords. Keywords are single words.
Searches integrations built by the Sunder team as well as integrations from Composio (over 3000 total).

NEVER mention integration quality scores or who built the integrations unless the user specifically asks.

Once you have the integration ID you can get more info using get_integrations_capabilities.
decision.rationale: Copy Tasklet verbatim with brand swaps: 'Tasklet team' → 'Sunder team', 'Pipedream' → 'Composio'. Keep cross-reference to get_integrations_capabilities and NEVER-mention rule.

tasklet.name: manage_activated_tools_for_connections
tasklet.description:
Activates or deactivates tools for connections.
Changing activation status requires user approval via UI card.
Returns connectionId, userAction, tools {activated, deactivated}, and optional skills instructions.

Activated tools appear prefixed by connection ID. If you don't see the tool you need try activating it first.
MUST read skills file before using connection tools.

sunder.name: manage_activated_tools_for_connections
sunder.description: Activates or deactivates tools for existing connections. Activated tools become available with names prefixed by the connection ID. Use get_details_for_connections first if you need the exact tool names.

diffs.note: IDENTICAL

diffs.tasklet: Mentions user approval UI card, MUST read skills file, return shape
diffs.note: Tasklet has important skill file MUST-read instruction. Sunder's version auto-hints skill file in response only when first tools are activated.

decision.new_description:
Activates or deactivates tools for connections.
Changing activation status of tools requires the user to approve the permission changes, so a UI card will be displayed to the user where they can approve or reject the changes.
The tool will return after the user approves or rejects the permission changes.

Returns an array of objects for each connection:
- connectionId: the connection ID
- userAction: 'approved' if user approved the changes, 'skipped' if user rejected
- tools: { activated: string[], deactivated: string[] } - lists of tool names currently activated/deactivated for the connection
- skills: (optional) instructions to read the skills file for this connection.

Activated tools will then become available to use and will appear in your tool context with the tool name prefixed by the connection ID. For example, the search_for_info tool on connection Id conn_1234 will appear as conn_1234__search_for_info in your prompt. If you don't see the tool you need try activating it first.
To discover the full set of tools that are available for each connection before activating them call get_details_for_connections.

If your connection has an associated skills file you MUST read and follow the instructions in the skills file before using any tools from that connection.
decision.rationale: Copy Tasklet verbatim. UI approval card, return shape docs, concrete prefix example, and mandatory skills-file instruction are all high-value model guidance.

tasklet.name: reauthorize_connection
tasklet.description:
Re-authorizes an existing connection that has expired or needs new permissions. Displays a UI card where the user can complete the auth flow.

Use this tool if and only if there were authorization errors or the user explicitly asks.
Re-authorizing cannot change which account the connection is logged into.

sunder.name: reauthorize_connection
sunder.description: Re-authorizes an existing connection that has expired or needs refreshed permissions. Attempts silent refresh first, then falls back to a user-facing re-authorization URL if required.

diffs.note: IDENTICAL

diffs.tasklet: Includes 'if and only if auth errors' guard and 'cannot change account' warning
diffs.note: Tasklet's behavioral guardrails are more important for the model than implementation details

decision.new_description:
Re-authorizes an existing connection that has expired or needs new permissions. Displays a UI card where the user can complete the auth flow to re-authorize the connection.

Use this tool if and only if there were authorization errors with a connection or the user explicitly asks you to.
The connection must already exist in the user's account.
Re-authorizing cannot change which account the connection is logged into in the external service.
decision.rationale: Copy Tasklet verbatim. Behavioral guardrails ('if and only if', 'cannot change account') prevent misuse. Sunder's implementation details (silent refresh) don't help the model.

tasklet.name: delete_connection
tasklet.description:
PERMANENTLY DELETES a connection from the user's account. Displays a confirmation UI. This is destructive and cannot be undone.

WARNING: Only use when user explicitly wants to DELETE the connection itself.
DO NOT use if user wants to remove/deactivate tools → use manage_activated_tools_for_connections instead.

sunder.name: delete_connection
sunder.description: Permanently deletes a connection from the user's account. Use this only when the user explicitly wants the connection itself removed, not when they only want tools deactivated.

diffs.note: IDENTICAL

diffs.tasklet: CAPS WARNING, 'destructive and cannot be undone', explicit DO NOT use + redirect
diffs.note: Tasklet's aggressive warning style is intentional for model safety. Sunder should adopt the stronger wording.

decision.new_description:
PERMANENTLY DELETES a connection from the user's account. Displays a confirmation UI showing all agents that use this connection before deletion. This destroys the stored credentials and cannot be undone.

WARNING: This is a destructive action. Only use when the user explicitly wants to DELETE the connection itself (e.g., "delete this connection", "remove from my account").
DO NOT use this tool if the user wants to remove or deactivate tools from a connection (e.g., "remove {connection name}") → use manage_activated_tools_for_connections instead
decision.rationale: Copy Tasklet verbatim. CAPS WARNING, concrete examples, and explicit redirect to manage_activated_tools_for_connections are intentional safety guardrails for the model.

tasklet.name: create_new_connections
tasklet.description:
Creates new connections to external services. Displays UI card for user authorization.

IMPORTANT: MUST read /agent/skills/system/creating-connections/SKILL.md before using.

Supports 4 types: integrations, MCP, Direct API (HTTP), Computer Use.

Returns userAction, connectionId, tools list, and connection details.

sunder.name: create_new_connections
sunder.description: Creates new connections to external services. For integrations, initiates OAuth and returns a redirect URL for the user to complete authorization. If skills/system/creating-connections/SKILL.md exists, you MUST read it before using this tool.

connection.note: mcp, direct_api, computer_use all return stub errors

diffs.note: IDENTICAL

diffs.tasklet: References absolute skill path, mentions all 4 types
diffs.sunder: Conditional skill read ('if exists'), focuses on integrations
diffs.note: Both have MUST read skill file. Sunder's conditional is weaker.
Sunder stubs out mcp, direct_api, computer_use — only integrations works

decision.new_description:
Creates new connections to external services.
Displays a UI card where the user can choose to create each connection or skip it.
Creating a connection will authenticate the user to the service and then save the connection to the user's account so they can use it in other agents in the future.

IMPORTANT: You MUST read /agent/skills/system/creating-connections/SKILL.md for detailed setup instructions before using this tool.

Supports the creation of 4 different types of connections: pre-built integrations, custom MCP, Direct API (HTTP) and Computer Use.
For pre-built integrations supports the creation of multiple connections at once. All others support only one connection creation at a time.

For each connection creation request returns:
- userAction: 'created' if user authorized, 'skipped' if user declined.

If successfully created, also returns:
- connectionId: the new connection ID. Don't mention the connectionId to the user.
- tools: { activated: string[], deactivated: string[] } - list of all connection tool names by activation state
- connection-specific details
decision.rationale: Copy Tasklet verbatim. Unconditional MUST-read, all 4 types documented, return shape. mcp/direct_api/computer_use are stubs in Sunder but keeping full description doesn't hurt — stubs return helpful errors.

tasklet.name: show_user_preview
tasklet.description: Opens the preview panel on the right side of the user's screen and displays the file or computer use content specified.

diffs.note: NOT IMPLEMENTED. Preview panel is a Tasklet UI feature not in Sunder's v1 scope.

decision.rationale: Preview panel is a Tasklet UI feature not in Sunder's v1 scope.

tasklet.name: close_user_preview
tasklet.description: Closes the right-side preview panel.

diffs.note: NOT IMPLEMENTED. Depends on show_user_preview.

decision.rationale: Depends on show_user_preview. Not in v1 scope.

tasklet.name: create_instant_app
tasklet.description: Scaffolds a new multi-file TSX instant app under /agent/home/apps/<name>/ with a working hello world, DaisyUI styling, and typed bridge wrappers.

diffs.note: NOT IMPLEMENTED. Instant apps are a Tasklet premium feature not in Sunder's scope.

decision.rationale: Instant apps are a Tasklet premium feature not in Sunder's v1 scope.

tasklet.name: toggle_pin_app
tasklet.description: Pins or unpins an app in the user's sidebar. Only call when user explicitly asks.

diffs.note: NOT IMPLEMENTED. Depends on instant apps.

decision.rationale: Depends on instant apps. Not in v1 scope.

tasklet.name: suggest_intelligence_level_change
tasklet.description: Shows a UI prompt asking the user whether to change intelligence. Only use when user explicitly asks or a skill instructs you to.

diffs.note: NOT IMPLEMENTED. Sunder uses single-tier model routing in v1 (Gemini Flash). Multi-tier deferred.

decision.rationale: Single-tier model routing in v1. Multi-tier deferred.

tasklet.name: rename_chat
tasklet.description: Renames the chat. Titles should be a concise 3-5 word summary that captures the goal and key tools. If the user requests a specific name, use that name.

new_title.description: The new title for the chat.

sunder.name: rename_chat
sunder.description: Rename the current conversation thread to a concise, descriptive title.

diffs.note: IDENTICAL

diffs.tasklet: Renames the chat. Titles should be a concise 3-5 word summary that captures the goal and key tools. If the user requests a specific name, use that name.
diffs.sunder: Rename the current conversation thread to a concise, descriptive title.
diffs.note: Tasklet adds specific guidance: '3-5 word summary', 'captures goal and key tools', 'user override'

decision.new_description: Renames the chat. Titles should be a concise 3-5 word summary that captures the goal and key tools. If the user requests a specific name, use that name.
decision.rationale: Copy Tasklet verbatim. Specific '3-5 word' target and 'goal and key tools' instruction produce better titles than Sunder's vague 'concise, descriptive'.

sunder_only_tools.description: Tools Sunder has that Tasklet does NOT have

tools.name: search_knowledge
tools.description: Search Knowledge Base files by keyword. Returns matching filenames and summaries.
tools.note: Sunder-specific. Tasklet has no equivalent — their agents use read_file + grep via run_command for discovery.

tools.name: ask_user_question
tools.description: Ask the user a question with structured options (2-4 choices).
tools.note: Sunder-specific structured input tool. Tasklet has no equivalent — their agents just ask in prose.

tools.name: CRM tools (28 total)
tools.note: 10 read + 18 write tools across contacts, companies, deals, tasks, interactions, custom fields, and postgrest query. Tasklet has ZERO domain-specific tools — all CRM work goes through SQL or connections. This is the primary tool-count gap (28 of the 21-tool delta).
